title: Zag
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Theta-288.jpg]] |name=Zag
RC-2088 |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|3|366|Zag (RC-2088)}} |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]]{{Tales|22|Honor Bound}} |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Tan |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **A [[Unidentified clone commando team (Asturias)|clone commando team]] **[[Aiwha Squad]]{{CWACite|4|Orders (comic story)}} |masters= |apprentices= }} '''Zag''', or '''RC-2088''', was a [[Clone commando/Legends|Clone commando]] and [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]] of a [[Unidentified clone commando team (Asturias)|team of clone commandos]]. Later, after the rest of his team was killed in action, he was reassigned to [[Aiwha Squad]]. ==Biography== During the height of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], RC-2088's team was assigned to [[Action on Asturias|protect]] [[Lorca Oviedo]] en route to a conference on [[Aviles Prime]], accompanying him in their [[CR25 troop carrier/Legends|CR25 troop carrier]]. Oviedo was a prominent industrialist loyal to the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], who had obtained a contract to build and ship weaponry for the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. This was part of a plan to expose levels of troop strength and deployment to [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]]. When the convoy was en route back from Aviles Prime, Oviedo's [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|Republic Cruiser]] reported a life support system failure, requiring that the cruiser and the clone escort drop out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. They came out near [[Asturias]], an abandoned factory moon, hoping to repair Oviedo's ship. As soon as they exited hyperspace, however, a [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|droid control ship]] attacked them, severely damaging both ships and causing them to crash land on Asturias. Of the eighteen man group of clones aboard the CR25, only seven survived the crash. Deciding they had to continue their mission, they made their way to the crash site of Oviedo's ship. There they made a shocking discovery - not only was the ship undamaged in the attack and had not crashed at all, its life support systems had never failed in the first place. The crew of the ship had been executed, and the clones found [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] footprints, along with those of a single human, leading away from the ship. Following the footsteps, they discovered Oviedo speaking with Wat Tambor via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]]. The Separatist attack was a setup, facilitated to create the illusion that Oviedo had not defected from the Republic, but rather was captured. As the droids and Oviedo believed all the clones to have perished, the commandos used the element of surprise to destroy the B1s and capture Oviedo. They then were forced to run a gauntlet of attacks on their way back to Oviedo's shuttle, fending off B1 and [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]], [[Droideka/Legends|Droidekas]], and [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droids]], all the while losing man after man. RC-2088's helmet was knocked off and his face seriously scarred in one attack, but he managed to get Oviedo aboard the ship and escape to Coruscant. Unfortunately, Oviedo would die under mysterious circumstances once he landed. Later, RC-2088 reported the outcome of his mission to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], who commended him for his efforts and told him his fellow clones' sacrifice would not go unnoticed. When he had left, Yoda expressed concern that the RC-2088 and all the other clones' loyalty may not always be to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. [[File:Zag.jpg|thumb|left|150px|2088 with [[Aiwha Squad]].]] After that, 2088 joined [[Aiwha Squad]] and participated in the [[Battle of Garqi (Clone Wars)|Battle of Garqi]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. When clones accidentally destroyed a Garqian farmhouse, RC-2088 and other clones were sent to rescue any survivors. His squad found a small boy, named [[Evan]]. After bringing the boy back to the Republic outpost, Jedi Master [[Traavis]] congratulated the clones on their rescue, and told them of the death of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], saying the end of the war was near. Suddenly, the clones received [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], and executed Traavis. Yoda's fears over the clone's loyalty had come true. ==Equipment== Generally, Zag wore the [[Katarn-class commando armor/Legends|''Katarn''-class commando armor]], although he has at times worn a variant of the [[cupid clone trooper]] armor that possessed white gauntlets, leg armor, and chest armor, as well as green fore-arm armor, shoulder armor, waist armor, and shin armor, a white and green helmet with a yellow visor, and dark wing-like ornaments jutting out of the backpack. ==Behind the scenes== "[[Honor Bound]]" first identified this clone as "R.C. Sergeant Theta Twenty-eight-eight." ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' later clarified the clone's designation as "RC-2088", while also establishing him as the clone "Zag" appearing in [[Orders (comic story)|''Orders'']]. The ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' issue that included Honor Bound was re-released on January 2008 as part of the Action Figure Comic Packs series. This pack included 2 action figures: a Super Battle Droid and an unnamed Republic Commando which could very well be RC-2088 himself. [[File:Zag.png|thumb|right|Clone commando Zag in ''Clone Wars Adventures''.]] The commando figure is based on the same mold as those of [[Scorch/Legends|Scorch]] and the [[Delta Squad/Legends|Delta Squad]] seven-figure pack. The pack itself can be seen at [http://shop.starwars.com/catalog/product.xml?product_id=414540;category_id=384 StarWarsShop.com] ==Appearances== *{{Tales|22|Honor Bound}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (video game)|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'']] {{Cosmetic}} *{{CWACite|4|Orders (comic story)}} {{C|as "Zag"}} ==Sources== *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|packtype=''Star Wars'' Comic Pack|pack=#16: Clone Commando and Super Battle Droid (''Star Wars Tales'' #22)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TACcomicpacks16.asp}} {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|99|Jawa's Corner}} {{1stID|Omega-288}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{1stID|RC-2088 and Zag}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=RC-2088 |nl=Zag |pl=RC-2088 |pt=RC-2088 }} [[Category:Clone commandos]] [[Category:Clone trooper sergeants]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
